bullets.headline: NVDA Q1 FY26: $44.1B revenue +69% YoY despite $4.5B H20 charge
bullets.summary: Q1 fiscal 2026 revenue $44.1B (+69% YoY, +12% QoQ); non-GAAP EPS $0.81. Q2 guidance $45B (+/-2%) absorbing ~$8B headwind from new H20 China export controls plus a $4.5B Q1 inventory charge.

bullets.headline: AMD MI450X could approach NVL144 parity in late 2026
bullets.summary: SemiAnalysis competitive analysis: AMD's MI450X rack-scale solution could challenge NVIDIA's VR200 NVL144 in late 2026, but AMD software still lags. NVIDIA's Blackwell Ultra / GB300 NVL72 / Rubin roadmap remains the supply-chain benchmark.

bullets.headline: BOTZ holds NVIDIA at 8.21% — cross-wave overlap noted
bullets.summary: BOTZ (a tracked holding) lists NVIDIA as its 4th-largest holding at 8.21% of NAV, confirming cross-wave exposure: NVIDIA's chips appear in industrial robots, autonomous vehicles, and humanoid platforms.

bullets.headline: GOOGL Q1 2026: revenue $109.9B (+22%); Cloud +63%, backlog $460B
bullets.summary: Alphabet Q1 2026 revenue $109.9B (+22% YoY), net income $62.6B (+81%). Google Cloud grew 63%; backlog nearly doubled QoQ to over $460B. Pichai: Cloud revenue 'would have been higher if we could meet demand.' 2026 capex guidance raised to $180-190B.

bullets.headline: Google Willow open for external research; neutral-atom expansion announced
bullets.summary: Google Quantum AI is accepting proposals (deadline May 15, 2026) for early external Willow access. Separately, Google revealed an expansion into neutral-atom quantum computing under Dr. Adam Kaufman, broadening its platform bets beyond superconducting qubits.

bullets.headline: Google TPU v7 picks up Anthropic and Meta as external customers
bullets.summary: SemiAnalysis: TPU v7 (Sunfish/Zebrafish) order revisions marked up sharply, with Anthropic and Meta confirmed as major external TPU customers. Gemini 3 trains primarily on TPUs, supporting GOOGL's infrastructure independence from NVIDIA.

bullets.headline: MSFT FY26 Q3: $77.7B (+18%); Azure +40%; bookings +112%
bullets.summary: Microsoft FY26 Q3 (calendar Q1 2026) revenue $77.7B (+18%), Azure +40%, commercial bookings +112%. EPS $3.72 GAAP / $4.13 non-GAAP (+23%). Capex $34.9B in the quarter; management flagged near-term gross margin compression from AI infrastructure scale-out.

bullets.headline: Microsoft reverses 2024-2025 datacenter pause; pursuing FM independence from OpenAI
bullets.summary: SemiAnalysis: after a 2024-2025 datacenter pause, Microsoft has reversed course and is 'pulling the trigger on everything it can get its hands on' for near-term capacity. Pursuing vertical integration by training its own foundation model to reduce OpenAI margin dependency.

bullets.headline: MSFT 2026 Quantum Pioneers Program; Denmark lab on track for late 2026
bullets.summary: Microsoft opened applications for its 2026 Quantum Pioneers Program, targeting measurement-based computing. Denmark quantum lab expansion on track for late 2026 operations. CEO described quantum as the 'next big accelerator in cloud.'

bullets.headline: Neutron debut delayed to Q4 2026 after January tank rupture
bullets.summary: Neutron Stage 1 propellant tank ruptured on Jan 21 during hydrostatic test; root cause was a hand-laid autoclave joint by a third-party contractor. Production shifted to automated fiber placement (AFP). First launch pushed to Q4 2026.

bullets.headline: RKLB 2026 guide: 25+ Electron launches; $1.85B record backlog
bullets.summary: Despite the Neutron setback, RKLB guided 20%+ Electron launch growth (25+ launches vs 21 in 2025). Q1 2026 earnings May 7; analyst consensus ~$191M revenue (50%+ YoY). $1.85B backlog (+73% YoY) provides visibility.

bullets.headline: Neutron picked for USAF cargo experiment; first commercial constellation customer signed
bullets.summary: Neutron selected for a USAF rapid global cargo delivery experiment (no earlier than 2026). RKLB signed its first Neutron commercial constellation customer (two launches mid-2026 / 2027). Mynaric (optical comms) and Geost+OSI acquisitions deepen vertical integration.

bullets.headline: BOTZ YTD -2.2%; $3.52B AUM; NVDA at 8.21% concentration
bullets.summary: BOTZ YTD ~-2.2% as of April 10, 2026; net assets $3.52B. Top: ABB 8.88%, Keyence 8.82%, Fanuc 8.34%, NVIDIA 8.21%, Intuitive Surgical 6.95%. 62 holdings spanning industrial automation, non-industrial robots, AVs.

bullets.headline: Robotics 'inflection point' per Robot Report 2026 industry survey
bullets.summary: Robot Report 2026 State of Robotics: industry at 'inflection point' as the conversation shifts from theoretical capability to real-world reliability. Constraints: safety, cost, regulation, deployment timelines. 12-24 month outlook flagged for select segments; warns where 'inflated expectations may diminish.'

bullets.headline: Humanoid hype dominates CES 2026 robotics coverage
bullets.summary: IEEE Spectrum CES 2026 coverage: a flood of humanoid robot demos, with humanoid hype the top robotics story of 2025 setting up elevated expectations for 2026 deployments.

bullets.headline: Intellia first-ever Phase 3 in-vivo CRISPR success: HAE attack rate -87%
bullets.summary: Intellia (ARKG top-10) reported first-ever Phase 3 win for in vivo CRISPR on April 27, 2026: lonvoctocogene-z reduced hereditary angioedema attack rate 87% vs placebo, with 62% of recipients attack-free. Rolling FDA NDA filing initiated. Wave-defining milestone for in vivo gene editing.

bullets.headline: CRISPR Therapeutics Q1 2026; FDA platform-therapy framework guidance
bullets.summary: CRISPR Therapeutics (ARKG largest holding, 9.71%) reported Q1 2026 May 4 and received FDA clearance of a new IND expanding an ongoing US trial. FDA Feb 2026 draft guidance on a 'plausible mechanism framework' could let one trial validate a platform across multiple targets - structural tailwind for ARKG core.

bullets.headline: ARK rotated into Beam, Twist, Intellia for 2026; ARKG 1y +33.7%
bullets.summary: ARK entered 2026 adding Beam, Twist, Intellia; tilting ARKG toward gene editing and base editing. ARKG YTD +4.6%, 1-year +33.7%, share price ~$29.30 mid-April 2026. AUM ~$1.04B.

bullets.headline: QTUM crosses $4B AUM; +77% 1y; 5-star Morningstar; rebalanced to hardware
bullets.summary: QTUM surpassed $4B AUM and earned 5-star Morningstar rating April 27, 2026. ~+77% trailing year, +20.5% YTD. March 20, 2026 rebalance shifted exposure from software-heavy names toward specialized quantum hardware providers.

bullets.headline: Quantinuum (Honeywell) IPO planned for 2026 - potential re-rating catalyst
bullets.summary: Honeywell's planned IPO of Quantinuum (per January 2026 filings) seen as a re-rating catalyst for every pure-play quantum holding. Quantinuum currently private; an IPO would add a large-cap pure-play and likely reshape QTUM's index weights.

bullets.headline: QCR 2026 outlook: hardware milestones likely; no commercial scale this year
bullets.summary: Quantum Computing Report 2026 predictions: continued hardware milestones (Willow external proposals; Microsoft Majorana 1) but no commercial deployment at scale this year. Quantum Echoes algorithm demonstrated verifiable advantage on Willow.

bullets.headline: NUKZ predominantly fission/fuel/utilities; pure fusion exposure indirect
bullets.summary: NUKZ holds 54 names across advanced reactors, utilities, construction/services, fuel. Top: Cameco 9.85%, GE Vernova 3.70%, Talen Energy 3.12%, Constellation Energy 2.82%. Pure fusion exposure indirect at best. Trading $72-73 early May 2026; 52-week range $42.10-$75.03.

bullets.headline: IEA 2026 sets 'first fusion plant by 2030' benchmark
bullets.summary: IEA State of Energy Innovation 2026 report (highlighted by Fusion Industry Association): a 'race to develop commercial fusion energy spurring new strategies and funding.' Private investment and government backing both grew through 2025. Benchmark: 'first fusion plant' demonstrating saleable energy by 2030.

bullets.headline: NUKZ -11.2% in March 2026; digesting prior nuclear-enthusiasm overshoot
bullets.summary: NUKZ returned -11.2% in March 2026, well behind Miscellaneous Sector category average -6.0%. The 52-week range ($42.10-$75.03) shows the name ran hard off lows but remains well below peak - consistent with a digestion phase since late 2024.

bullets.headline: AGG -1.7% in March 2026 vs category -1.8%; Morningstar Gold
bullets.summary: AGG returned -1.7% in March 2026 (relative grade A vs intermediate core bond category -1.8%). $138B AUM; trailing yield 3.93% vs category average 4.34%. Morningstar Gold as of April 30, 2026.

bullets.headline: AGG yield trails category; ~6yr duration is the primary risk
bullets.summary: Below-category yield (3.93% vs 4.34%) reflects Treasury and agency MBS mix alongside investment-grade corporates. Rising rates the primary risk: ~6yr duration means each 100bps rate hike implies ~6% price decline. No structural changes in the lookback window.

bullets.headline: BIL trailing 1Y 3.67% return; tracks ultra-short Treasuries near risk-free rate
bullets.summary: BIL tracks 1-3 month T-bills, delivering 3.67% total return TTM - essentially the risk-free rate. Fund near highest level since Nov 2007 (~$91.58). Yield tracks Fed funds rate closely.

bullets.headline: BIL yield ~4.3-4.5% competes with AGG's 3.93% on risk-adjusted basis
bullets.summary: BIL's cash-proxy role gains relative appeal when equity volatility rises. With longer-term yields elevated and the Fed on hold, BIL's ~4.3-4.5% annualized T-bill yield competes meaningfully with AGG's 3.93% trailing yield, with near-zero duration risk.

bullets.headline: IAU $86.72 May 1; ~17% pullback from $104.40 intraday peak
bullets.summary: IAU hit intraday 52-week high $104.40 earlier in 2026 before pulling back. Closing price May 1, 2026 was $86.72 - meaningful correction from peak. 52-week low $59.71. AUM $83.8B - the second-largest gold ETF.

bullets.headline: Gold's TTM run +75% driven by safe-haven demand; speculative premium unwinding
bullets.summary: Gold's sharp move from $59 to over $104 (~75%) in TTM driven by macro safe-haven demand, central-bank accumulation, and dollar weakness. Pullback to ~$87 by May 1 suggests speculative premium unwinding, though structural central-bank demand remains elevated.

bullets.headline: VIG YTD +4.4%; 1y +20.3%; tracks ~340 dividend-growth names with tech tilt
bullets.summary: VIG YTD +4.4%, 1y +20.3% as of May 1, 2026. Tracks S&P US Dividend Growers Index (~340 US companies with 10+ yrs dividend growth). Tech is 23% (higher than most dividend ETFs); overweights consumer staples and healthcare vs broad market.

bullets.headline: VIG competes with bonds and cash for optimizer weight
bullets.summary: VIG had volatility earlier in 2026 before April's tech rally restored performance. As quality-tilted defensive equity, VIG competes with bonds and cash for optimizer weight: 1y +20.3% beats BIL's 3.67% and AGG's flat-to-slightly-negative recent showing, at the cost of higher equity drawdown risk.

AI.rationale: Real, compounding revenue: GOOGL Cloud +63% (demand exceeding supply), MSFT Azure +40% with bookings +112%, NVDA datacenter +69% YoY despite $8B China headwind. Hyperscaler capex (GOOGL $180-190B, MSFT $35B/qtr) reflects competing to buy capacity, not speculate on it. Valuations stretched but thesis grounded in real enterprise spend.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: RKLB Electron growing (25+ launches guided), record $1.85B backlog, but pre-profit GAAP and Neutron (the medium-lift catalyst) slipped to Q4 2026 after a tank rupture. Real commercial revenue but transformative program still pre-launch.

robotics.rationale: Robot Report 2026 calls the sector at 'inflection point' shifting from capability to reliability. CES 2026 dominated by humanoid demos. BOTZ slight YTD underperformance reflects industrial macro softness, but adoption trajectory is real and accelerating. Risk of tipping into peak if humanoid timelines disappoint.

engineered_biology.rationale: Intellia first-ever Phase 3 in-vivo CRISPR win (April 2026) is a genuine wave milestone. But ARKG only ~$1B AUM; 5y avg return -18.8% (long ARK digestion); 1y +33.7% is recovery off depressed levels, not new broad re-rating. FDA platform-therapy framework is structural tailwind. Transitioning from post-2021-bust recovery into buildup.

quantum.rationale: QTUM +77% TTM and $4B AUM reflect rising interest, but QCR 2026 says no commercial deployment at scale this year. March rebalance toward hardware specialists structurally raises beta to pre-revenue names. Quantinuum IPO would add real pricing signal but signals private-to-public transition. Buildup leaning toward early surge: financial markets pricing in future, hardware milestones arriving, commercial revenue not yet material.

nuclear_fusion.rationale: IEA 2026 acknowledges momentum and sets 2030 'first plant' benchmark. But NUKZ -11.2% in March and pullback from $75 peak shows prior nuclear enthusiasm digesting. Critically, NUKZ is primarily fission infrastructure, not pure fusion. The fusion wave itself is buildup - private investment growing, regulatory frameworks being built, but commercial energy still a decade away.

general_markets.rationale: AGG, BIL, IAU, VIG serve portfolio construction roles, not thematic bets. Gold (IAU) had a dramatic +75% TTM run with a sharp -17% pullback - macro-driven, not wave-driven. AGG and BIL are rate-sensitive. VIG is a new quality-equity defensive option. No wave stage assigned.

thematic_concerns.concern: NUKZ is bucketed under nuclear_fusion but its actual portfolio is dominated by fission utilities, nuclear fuel (Cameco), and energy infrastructure. True fusion exposure is negligible. The profile's nuclear_fusion thesis anticipates abundant low-cost plasma-confinement energy; NUKZ delivers uranium-cycle and fission-utility exposure instead. Treat as fission proxy until pure-play fusion firms IPO.
thematic_concerns.evidence: NUKZ holdings: Cameco 9.85%, GE Vernova 3.70%, Talen Energy 3.12%, Constellation Energy 2.82%. No pure-play fusion in top holdings.

thematic_concerns.concern: BOTZ holds NVIDIA at 8.21% as its 4th-largest position - meaning ~8% of BOTZ's NAV is already separately held as NVDA in the portfolio. Combined AI + robotics exposure is overstated by this overlap.
thematic_concerns.evidence: BOTZ holdings May 4, 2026 show NVIDIA at 8.21% of NAV.

thematic_concerns.concern: IAU has pulled back ~17% from its 2026 intraday $104.40 peak to $86.72. Gold at $83.8B AUM with +75% TTM is no longer the underowned safe haven it once was; speculative positioning may be unwinding.

watchlist_suggestions.rationale: RKLB is the sole rockets_spacecraft name and Neutron's Q4 2026 delay concentrates risk. SpaceX remains private; potential diversifiers spread launch-delay risk across the wave.

candidates.thesis_fit: Direct-to-cell satellite broadband; broadens space exposure beyond launch.
Lunar logistics and surface payloads; complementary to RKLB launch.
Diversified space-economy ETF; spreads single-name risk.

watchlist_suggestions.rationale: No pure-play fusion company is publicly traded. NUKZ is the closest proxy but is majority fission. Watch for Commonwealth Fusion Systems, TAE Technologies, and Helion (Microsoft offtake) reaching public markets; first listings could arrive 2027-2028.
